clinical_history: ECG from a middle-aged woman with palpitations shows which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The rhythm is intermittent sinus (with possible wandering atrial pacemaker since the P wave morphology varies subtly) with premature atrial complexes and, most important, short runs (bursts) of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (PAF). The patient underwent percutaneous pulmonary vein isolation/ablation therapy. Clear fibrillatory activity can be seen during the PAF episodes with atrial cycle rates >350/min. The maximum ventricular (QRS) response (at rest) is about 125/min during this short recording. Capturing short bursts of PAF in a 12-lead ECG is rare and predicts longer runs of this major arrhythmia during more sustained recordings (e.g., those obtained with event recorders).
